name: Tour de la Réserve Biologique de la Mailloueyre – running course
name_original: Tour de la Réserve Biologique de la Mailloueyre – course à pied
region_intro: Jogging routes around Mimizan traverse a diverse landscape of vast pine forests, expansive sandy beaches, and serene lakes. The region, situated on the Atlantic coast, offers varied terrain from flat coastal paths to shaded forest trails. Runners can explore the immense Landes forest, one of Europe's largest, or enjoy routes along the shores of Lake Aureilhan. The area's natural features provide a range of running experiences for different preferences. Best jogging routes around Mimizan The most popular jogging route is Tour de la Réserve Biologique de la Mailloueyre – running course, an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 30 minutes to complete. This route leads through diverse landscapes including forests, dunes, and marshes. Another top favourite among local runners is Courant de Mimizan Circuit, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This trail offers scenic views along the lake and through forested sections. Local runners also love the La Vélodyssée – Beach Boardwalk Access loop from Mimizan, an 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through coastal areas and along the Vélodyssée, often completed in about 1 hour 29 minutes. Jogging around Mimizan is defined by vast pine forests, sandy Atlantic beaches, and calm lake shores. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging runs. The routes in Mimizan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 3000 runners have used komoot to explore Mimizan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on running routes around Mimizan?
A: Mimizan offers a diverse range of terrains for runners. You can enjoy routes through the vast Landes pine forests, along expansive sandy Atlantic beaches, and beside the serene shores of Lake Aureilhan. Some trails also feature dunes and marshes, providing varied landscapes and running experiences.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Mimizan area?
A: There are over 120 running routes available around Mimizan, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include everything from easy strolls to more challenging runs through varied terrain.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Mimizan?
A: Yes, Mimizan offers several easy routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed run. For instance, sections along the &lt;a href="https://www.mimizan-tourisme.com/en/i-discover/a-varied-territory/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Promenade Fleurie&lt;/a&gt; along Lake Aureilhan provide a gentle, scenic option. Overall, there are 14 easy routes in the area.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or landmarks I can see while jogging in Mimizan?
A: Many routes offer beautiful scenery. You can explore the tranquil Landes forest, enjoy views of Lake Aureilhan, or run along the Atlantic beaches. Specific points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3757562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge to Flower Island&lt;/a&gt; on Lake Aureilhan, and coastal paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/525452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001191095/tour-de-la-reserve-biologique-de-la-mailloueyre-running-course" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour de la Réserve Biologique de la Mailloueyre&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, leads through forests, dunes, and marshes.

Q: Are there any circular running trails around Mimizan?
A: Yes, many routes in Mimizan are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001177698/courant-de-mimizan-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Courant de Mimizan Circuit&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views along the lake and through forested sections. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10607761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vélodyssée – Beach Boardwalk Access loop from Mimizan&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through coastal areas.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails in Mimizan?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking longer runs, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10607891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flower garden – Lac d'Aureilhan loop from Clocher-porche de Mimizan&lt;/a&gt; cover approximately 18.4 km. The Vélodyssée also offers sections suitable for extended runs, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35454249" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vélodyssée loop from Saint-Girons-en-Marensin&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Mimizan?
A: The running routes in Mimizan are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 40 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, from the quiet pine forests to the invigorating coastal paths, and the well-maintained trails that cater to various abilities.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running options in Mimizan?
A: Many of Mimizan's trails are suitable for families, especially those around Lake Aureilhan or within the Landes forest, which offer shaded and relatively flat paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.mimizan-tourisme.com/en/i-discover/a-varied-territory/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Promenade Fleurie&lt;/a&gt; is a particularly pleasant option for a gentle jog or walk with children.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Mimizan?
A: Mimizan is enjoyable for jogging throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, while summer mornings provide a cool and tranquil environment, especially in the shaded pine forests. The vast Landes forest is particularly appealing during warmer months for its natural shade.

Q: Are there any running routes that are accessible by public transport or have nearby parking?
A: Many routes in Mimizan are easily accessible. The town center and areas around Lake Aureilhan and Mimizan Plage typically offer parking facilities. For specific public transport options to trailheads, it's advisable to check local transport schedules, though many routes start close to residential or tourist areas.

Q: Can I run with my dog on the trails in Mimizan?
A: While many natural areas in Mimizan are dog-friendly, it's always best to check local regulations for specific trails, especially within protected areas or on beaches during peak seasons. Generally, the extensive forest paths offer great opportunities for running with your dog, provided they are kept under control.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Vélodyssée cycling path?
A: Yes, Mimizan is part of the Vélodyssée, and there are sections of this long-distance cycling route that are suitable for running. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10607761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vélodyssée – Beach Boardwalk Access loop from Mimizan&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate run along coastal areas and the Vélodyssée.
tips: Discover the dunes and coastal forests of the Mailloueyre Biological Reserve. A diverse mix of natural environments in which you will enjoy walking.
